text:
Market today: Mixed to cautious. Tech and chip names are struggling to bounce after a global AI-driven selloff (headline 9). The mood is fragile - Wall Street is dip-buying, but that consensus itself is a warning sign (headline 4). Your book is squarely in the crosshairs.

Your holdings: PLTR is down 12.77% and flagged SELL - no confirmed floor yet, watch that $85 stop hard. AVGO is your largest position, down 12.45% and flagged TRIM - chip weakness is a direct headwind here. SPAL is down 14.48% - it's a 2x leveraged ETF that decays daily; on margin, this one can spiral fast. SPCX is a brand-new, very volatile IPO with almost no price history - size it small. Margin amplifies every one of these drawdowns.

Watch: 1) Whether chip names stabilize or extend losses after the AI selloff - AVGO, SMH, CRWD all move with this. 2) AVGO specifically near its $336 stop - a break there with margin exposure is painful.

Bottom line: Trim or cut your losers before the market decides for you.
